title: SandboxAdapter trait + AdapterCapabilities struct (Rust trait contract)
goal: Define the canonical `SandboxAdapter` Rust trait in src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/adapter.rs with the exact eight methods named by spec §3.5.1 (spawn, exec, fs_bind, net_policy, kill, status, exit_code, capabilities) and the machine-readable `AdapterCapabilities` struct per spec §3.5.4. The trait is the single authority surface every Handshake-spawned process passes through; no adapter-specific types may leak into the public surface.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/adapter.rs holding the trait + AdapterCapabilities struct + capability enums; mod.rs re-exports; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/sandbox_adapter_trait_tests.rs (compile-time interface tests + a NoopAdapter test double).
unblock_conditions: MT-041 shared types merged; adapter trait can reference ProcessSpec/ProcessHandle/NetPolicy/Signal/ExecResult.
implementation_notes: Per spec §3.5.1 the trait surface is exactly: `async fn spawn(&self, spec: ProcessSpec) -> Result<ProcessHandle, SandboxAdapterError>`; `async fn exec(&self, handle: &ProcessHandle, cmd: Command) -> Result<ExecResult, SandboxAdapterError>`; `async fn fs_bind(&self, handle: &ProcessHandle, host_path: PathBuf, guest_path: PathBuf, mode: BindMode) -> Result<(), SandboxAdapterError>`; `async fn net_policy(&self, handle: &ProcessHandle, policy: NetPolicy) -> Result<(), SandboxAdapterError>`; `async fn kill(&self, handle: &ProcessHandle, signal: Signal) -> Result<(), SandboxAdapterError>`; `async fn status(&self, handle: &ProcessHandle) -> Result<ProcessStatus, SandboxAdapterError>`; `async fn exit_code(&self, handle: &ProcessHandle) -> Result<Option<i32>, SandboxAdapterError>`; `fn capabilities(&self) -> AdapterCapabilities` (sync; called frequently and must not block). The trait must be `Send + Sync` (every adapter is shared across roles per HBR-SWARM-001). Use `async-trait` crate (already a workspace dep per KERNEL-002) for the async surface. AdapterCapabilities struct per spec §3.5.4: `{ adapter_id: AdapterId, filesystem_isolation_strength: IsolationStrength, network_isolation_strength: IsolationStrength, gpu_passthrough: GpuPassthrough, stdio_throughput_class: ThroughputClass, win32_native_fidelity: bool, cross_machine_portable: bool }` where IsolationStrength={Weak,Strong,VeryStrong}, GpuPassthrough={None,NvidiaCuda,VendorAgnostic}, ThroughputClass={Low,Medium,High}. All derive Serialize+Deserialize+Clone+Debug. Add a `default_no_op_capabilities()` constructor for test doubles that returns IsolationStrength::Weak / GpuPassthrough::None / ThroughputClass::Low / false / false. Tests: (a) compile-time test that a `Box<dyn SandboxAdapter>` is constructible from a NoopAdapter (proves trait object safety), (b) NoopAdapter where spawn/exec/etc return SandboxAdapterError::AdapterUnavailable so unit-test consumers can wire the trait without a real isolation backend, (c) trait_send_sync_test verifies via type assertion that any SandboxAdapter impl is Send+Sync, (d) capabilities() returns a clonable AdapterCapabilities and serde round-trips, (e) lint test (grep test) verifies `pub trait SandboxAdapter` is exactly this 8-method shape (no method added or removed) to catch silent contract drift.
proof_commands: node -e "const f=require('fs').readFileSync('src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/adapter.rs','utf8'); const m=f.match(/pub trait SandboxAdapter[\s\S]*?\}/); if(!m) process.exit(1); for(const sym of ['spawn','exec','fs_bind','net_policy','kill','status','exit_code','capabilities']) { if(!m[0].includes(sym)) { console.error('missing',sym); process.exit(1);} }"
risk_if_missed: Without the trait fixed first, MT-043 registry, MT-044/046/047 adapters, and every B.2 integration MT cannot proceed; trait-shape drift between adapters becomes the most expensive defect class.
validator_focus: Trait surface is exactly the 8 methods named by spec §3.5.1 (no extra method, no renamed method); trait is object-safe; AdapterCapabilities fields match spec §3.5.4 verbatim; no adapter-specific crate appears in adapter.rs imports.

minimum_controls: Compile-time trait shape lint via proof command grep asserts the exact 8 spec-named methods are present.
Object-safety test asserts Box<dyn SandboxAdapter> compiles (prevents accidental generic method addition).
NoopAdapter is the ONLY test-double allowed; tests must not silently introduce a real adapter without going through MT-044/MT-046/MT-047.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: SandboxAdapter trait exposes exactly the eight spec-named methods, is Send+Sync via async-trait, and AdapterCapabilities matches §3.5.4 with a minor additive runtime_available field.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: SandboxAdapter trait exposes exactly the eight spec-named methods, is Send+Sync via async-trait, and AdapterCapabilities matches §3.5.4 with a minor additive runtime_available field.
